rows.notes: Top-centre title clearly written as 'Soluta'. No amounts on this line; heading is unambiguous.
Line immediately under title, clearly a structural label. Handwriting legible enough to read 'Rectori, Scholantibus et Officiaris'. No monetary figures on the line.
Year written in left margin. No amounts; treated as a header/section marker. Digits clearly '1801'.

Description and amounts align with right-hand vertical rules. Pounds '158' and shillings '14' clearly placed. Pence reads '6' with a trailing halfpenny loop (interpreted as 0.5). All values in valid ranges.
Abbreviated repeat line ('Do'). Pounds '188' and shillings '17' clearly aligned. Pence '6' with halfpenny tail visible; read as 6½d. Column alignment clear.

rows.description: Stipend. ad Test. Hativ. et Officiaris
rows.notes: Description partly abbreviated; amounts in right columns read as '20' pounds, '6' shillings, '8' pence. Pence shape clear but some ink variation on description reduces confidence slightly.
Pounds column shows '27' with dashes in s and d columns indicating 0s. Handwriting of description is legible enough to identify place names; monetary columns clearly '27 0s 0d'.
Amounts read as 33 pounds, 6 shillings, 8 pence. Description has abbreviation 'Dno.' and surname looks like 'Thiers' — legible enough for transcription. All currency values within valid ranges.
Pounds '60' clearly in pounds column; shillings and pence are dashed (interpreted as zeros). Description legible ('Augm. Com. et Capellanis').
Repeated 'ut supra' line. Numerical columns read as 30 pounds, 9 shillings, 2 pence; alignment and digits are clear.
New division entry. Pounds '89', shillings '9', pence '10' clearly aligned; handwriting of description readable.
Repeat line ('Do.'). Amounts match previous Merton line (89 9 10). Column alignment consistent and digits clear.

rows.description: Ad fest. Stipend. ad fest. Annuni
rows.notes: Description partially abbreviated; monetary columns read as 34 pounds, 13 shillings, 4 pence. The '13' in shillings is slightly compressed but within valid range (0–19).
Place name appears as 'Kidleighton' (handwritten). Values read as 38 pounds, 11 shillings, 3 pence. Shilling '11' clear though ink slightly blotchy.
Same 'ut supra' closing line as earlier; amounts identical to prior similar lines (30 9 2). Columns well aligned and legible.
Abbreviated description 'et Officiar...' but meaning clear in context. Monetary columns read '20 6 8' though ink on shillings/pence slightly faded causing moderate ambiguity.
Repeat-style line with surname 'Thiers'. Amounts read as 33/6/8; digits are consistent with earlier 'Thiers' line but ink has minor smearing.

rows.description: D. Ioan Augm. Com. Moderatori et Sub Decano
rows.notes: Description is long and somewhat cramped; the pounds column reads '56' clearly but the shillings and pence columns are dashes (interpreted as zeros). Slight uncertainty because faint marks could be small stray ink but 56/0/0 best fits alignment.

rows.description: Kidleighton Augm. pro l.ta parte anni
rows.notes: Same place name as earlier block. Amounts read as 12 pounds, 7 shillings, 1 pence; pence '1' is small but readable. Moderate confidence due to tight handwriting.
Repeated footer line 'ut supra' in this section. Amounts match other occurrences (30 9 2). Clear column alignment.

rows.description: Ad Gwinnear Stipend. ad Test. Michl.
rows.notes: Pounds '27' clearly visible with dashes for shillings and pence. Description reads as an augmentation for 'Gwinnear' and 'Michl.' abbreviation; monetary columns unambiguous.

Pounds '33' and shillings '15' are clear. Pence appears as '1' with a small trailing halfpenny mark (interpreted as 1½d). The fraction sign is faint; chosen 0.5 (halfpenny) based on looped stroke after the '1'. Slight ambiguity reduces confidence.
Another 'ut supra' closing; amounts 30/9/2 match other occurrences, alignment and digits clear.
Section header for scholars; handwriting a little ornate ('Scholani ... Regis ... Maj.r Duprè') which introduces the two small scholar lines below. No monetary amounts on this header line.

Short scholar entry. Description read as 'Iesus Coll: Time' (handwritten abbreviations). Monetary columns read '1' pound, '3' shillings, '1' pence; digits are small and somewhat faint, producing moderate uncertainty.

Second scholar entry; amount appears as '3' pounds, '1' shilling, '6' pence with a small halfpenny loop after the 6 (interpreted as 6½d). The halfpenny mark is faint but visible; description abbreviated as 'Do.' (ditto).

Final grand total line at bottom right reads '1049' pounds, '10' shillings, '6' pence with a halfpenny mark (interpreted as 6½d). The total is clearly separated by an underline; digits are well-formed though the halfpenny sign is slightly faint.
